## Patch release: pkexec dialog never appearing

**Bug.** On any v0.2.0 install, `pkexec`-based authentication never showed
the Sentinel dialog. Polkit silently fell through to the cosmic-osd
password prompt and `sudo` (when wired) showed the dialog correctly only
because of an unrelated path. The same affected COSMIC Settings, mount
operations, and any other polkit-mediated action.

**Cause.** `pam_sentinel.so` used `pamh.get_user(None)` to identify the
user whose Wayland display to detect. polkit's PAM service doesn't
propagate `PAM_USER` reliably (the `pam-bindings` wrapper returned
`Err(PAM_SUCCESS)`), so we hit the `PAM_USER_UNKNOWN` early-return path
and polkit fell through to its registered authentication agent.

**Fix.** `pam_sentinel.so` now identifies the calling user via
`/proc/<ppid>/loginuid` (the canonical Linux audit answer to "who is the
real human behind this auth?"). `loginuid` is set by login/PAM at session
start and immune to setuid transitions through `sudo`/`pkexec`. Falls
back to `/proc/<ppid>/status` real-uid, then to our own real uid, in
order. The display-detection and helper-spawn paths now consistently
target the requesting user, which is who'll actually see the dialog.

**Affects.** Anyone on v0.2.0 attempting `pkexec`. Direct `sudo` users
were not affected.

## Heads up: dual UI on pkexec is *not* fixed in this release

After v0.2.1 you'll see *two* dialogs on `pkexec`: Sentinel's, plus the
cosmic polkit agent's password prompt rendered eagerly in parallel.
Clicking **Allow** on Sentinel ends the PAM stack early and polkit
cancels the cosmic prompt, but the duplicate-render race is annoying.

The proper fix is making Sentinel itself the registered polkit agent.
Tracked as v0.3.0 in
[#1](https://github.com/atayozcan/sentinel/issues/1).

## Upgrade

```bash
yay -Syu sentinel        # AUR
sudo apt install ./sentinel_0.2.1-1_amd64.deb
sudo dnf install ./sentinel-0.2.1-1.x86_64.rpm

# or rebuild from source:
git pull && pkexec ./install.sh
```
